citations.description: Ticket comments reviewed via show_ticket: only five comments present; none record a `rebar criteria eval project.portability --runs 3 --output json` invocation or its output with the required metrics.

findings.criterion: `indigoid-unmystic-serpent` records three live eval runs over `PORT-F1`–`PORT-F4` plus `PORT-N1`–`PORT-N4` with recall `1.0`, false-accept rate `0.0`, agreement `1.0`, per-case stability of at least two of three, and Cohen kappa of at least `0.70`. [operator-attested]
findings.detail: This is an operator-attested criterion. No ticket comment records a completed live run of `rebar criteria eval project.portability --runs 3 --output json` with the required threshold metrics (recall 1.0, false-accept 0.0, agreement 1.0, per-case stability ≥ 2/3, kappa ≥ 0.70). The only experiment-related comment in the ticket is the worktree experiment (timestamp 2026-07-16T00:50:08Z), which covers the structural wiring proof (Pass-1 fan-in + Pass-4 move applicability/rendering) — not the semantic live-eval calibration runs. The five required numeric thresholds are stated in the docs and tests but there is no recorded command output or run artifact in the ticket satisfying the concrete-attestation bar.
findings.remediation: Run `rebar criteria eval project.portability --runs 3 --output json` against the live criterion and record the output (recall, false_accept, agreement, per-case stability, and kappa values) as a ticket comment naming the run timestamp, the exact command, and the numeric results.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
